source_commit: 6e5d1b4 task6-ddr3: prepare v96 single-driver WDF probe
interpretation: v96 fixed the v95 multi-driver issue and produced a valid board result. Native write-data push does occur, so the generated-core push signal is not completely wrong. At the captured native PHY write-data event, native selection is active, slave/master write-data events and pop are asserted, but the FIFO is empty and there is no concurrent push. The native WDF FIFO is being drained before the observed PHY write-data event, most likely because the pop condition is tied to the LiteDRAM slave wrdata event rather than the actual PHY/master wrdata event, or because the slave event fires earlier/more often than the PHY consumes data.
next_action: For v97, keep the single-driver patch and compact status byte, but change the FIFO pop condition from native slave wrdata_en to native master wrdata_en, or otherwise guard against popping when empty. The goal is to keep accepted native WDF data resident until the actual PHY write-data event.
